name: Rosalind Franklin
tagline: The Woman Behind DNA's Double Helix
bio: Rosalind Franklin's X-ray diffraction image known as Photo 51 was the crucial evidence that revealed the double helix structure of DNA. Her colleague Maurice Wilkins showed the photograph to James Watson without her knowledge, and Watson and Crick used it to build their famous model. Franklin never knew her data had been shared. She died of ovarian cancer at 37, four years before Watson, Crick, and Wilkins received the Nobel Prize — which is not awarded posthumously.
bioCn: 罗莎琳德·富兰克林的X射线衍射图像——著名的Photo 51——是揭示DNA双螺旋结构的关键证据。她的同事莫里斯·威尔金斯在她不知情的情况下将照片展示给了詹姆斯·沃森，沃森和克里克利用它构建了他们著名的模型。富兰克林从不知道她的数据被分享了。她37岁死于卵巢癌，在沃森、克里克和威尔金斯获得诺贝尔奖的四年前——该奖不授予已故者。
achievements: Captured Photo 51, key evidence for DNA's structure
Pioneered X-ray diffraction studies of biological molecules
Also made major contributions to understanding viruses
achievementsCn: 拍摄Photo 51，DNA结构的关键证据
开创了生物分子的X射线衍射研究
对理解病毒也做出了重大贡献
quote: Science and everyday life cannot and should not be separated.
quoteCn: 科学与日常生活不能也不应该分离。
references: Maddox, Brenda. Rosalind Franklin: The Dark Lady of DNA. HarperCollins, 2002.

name: Dorothy Hodgkin
taglineCn: X射线晶体学诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Dorothy Crowfoot Hodgkin advanced the technique of X-ray crystallography to determine the three-dimensional structures of biomolecules. She confirmed the structure of penicillin, mapped vitamin B12, and later determined the structure of insulin — work that took her 35 years. She received the 1964 Nobel Prize in Chemistry, becoming only the third woman to receive the prize. She was also a lifelong peace activist.
bioCn: 多萝西·克劳福特·霍奇金推进了X射线晶体学技术，用以确定生物分子的三维结构。她确认了青霉素的结构、绘制了维生素B12的图谱，后来还确定了胰岛素的结构——这项工作耗费了她35年。她获得了1964年诺贝尔化学奖，成为第三位获此殊荣的女性。她也是一位终身和平活动家。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (1964)
Determined structures of penicillin, vitamin B12, and insulin
Only British woman to win a Nobel Prize in science
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔化学奖（1964年）
确定了青霉素、维生素B12和胰岛素的结构
唯一获得诺贝尔科学奖的英国女性
quote: I was captured for life by chemistry and by crystals.
quoteCn: 化学和晶体俘获了我的一生。
references: Ferry, Georgina. Dorothy Hodgkin: A Life. Granta Books, 1998.

name: Alice Ball
tagline: Developed the First Effective Leprosy Treatment
taglineCn: 开发了第一种有效的麻风病治疗方法
bio: Alice Ball was an African-American chemist who developed the first effective treatment for leprosy (Hansen's disease) at the age of 23. Her 'Ball Method' made chaulmoogra oil injectable by isolating its active compounds, treating thousands of patients worldwide. She died at just 24 from chlorine poisoning in a laboratory accident. Her work was initially claimed by a colleague, but her contributions were formally recognized by the University of Hawaii in 2000.
bioCn: 爱丽丝·鲍尔是一位非裔美国化学家，23岁时开发了第一种有效的麻风病（汉森病）治疗方法。她的'鲍尔方法'通过分离活性化合物使大风子油可注射，在全球治疗了数千名患者。她年仅24岁因实验室氯气中毒去世。她的工作最初被一位同事占为己有，但2000年夏威夷大学正式承认了她的贡献。
achievements: Developed the Ball Method for treating leprosy
Youngest and first African-American master's graduate at University of Hawaii
Treated thousands of leprosy patients worldwide
achievementsCn: 开发了治疗麻风病的鲍尔方法
夏威夷大学最年轻的、也是第一位非裔美国硕士毕业生
在全球治疗了数千名麻风病患者
references: Swaby, Rachel. Headstrong: 52 Women Who Changed Science. Crown/Broadway, 2015.

name: Irène Joliot-Curie
taglineCn: 人工放射性诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Irène Joliot-Curie, daughter of Marie and Pierre Curie, shared the 1935 Nobel Prize in Chemistry with her husband Frédéric for their discovery of artificial radioactivity. She demonstrated that radioactive elements could be artificially produced, opening the door to nuclear medicine and advancing nuclear physics. Like her mother, she died from leukemia caused by prolonged radiation exposure. She also served in the French government as undersecretary of state for scientific research.
bioCn: 伊雷娜·约里奥-居里是玛丽和皮埃尔·居里的女儿，与丈夫弗雷德里克因发现人工放射性而分享了1935年诺贝尔化学奖。她证明放射性元素可以人工制造，为核医学打开了大门，推动了核物理学的发展。和她的母亲一样，她死于长期辐射暴露引起的白血病。她还在法国政府担任科学研究国务副秘书。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (1935) for artificial radioactivity
Part of the only mother-daughter Nobel Prize pair
Served as French undersecretary of state for scientific research
achievementsCn: 因人工放射性获诺贝尔化学奖（1935年）
唯一的母女诺贝尔奖得主之一
担任法国科学研究国务副秘书

name: Gertrude Elion
bio: Gertrude Elion developed a revolutionary approach to drug design that used differences in biochemistry between normal cells and pathogens. Her method led to treatments for leukemia, herpes, malaria, gout, and organ transplant rejection. She shared the 1988 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine. Despite never completing a PhD — she was denied admission to graduate programs because she was a woman — her innovations saved millions of lives.
bioCn: 格特鲁德·埃利恩开发了一种革命性的药物设计方法，利用正常细胞和病原体之间的生化差异。她的方法导致了白血病、疱疹、疟疾、痛风和器官移植排斥反应的治疗方案。她分享了1988年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖。尽管从未完成博士学位——她因为是女性而被拒绝进入研究生项目——她的创新挽救了数百万人的生命。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (1988)
Developed treatments for leukemia, herpes, and malaria
Revolutionized rational drug design methodology
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（1988年）
开发了白血病、疱疹和疟疾的治疗方法
革新了理性药物设计方法论

name: Jennifer Doudna
taglineCn: CRISPR基因编辑的共同发明者
bio: Jennifer Doudna and Emmanuelle Charpentier shared the 2020 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for developing CRISPR-Cas9, a revolutionary tool for editing DNA with unprecedented precision. Their discovery has transformed biology, enabling potential cures for genetic diseases, creation of disease-resistant crops, and new approaches to treating cancer. It is one of the most consequential scientific discoveries of the 21st century.
bioCn: 珍妮弗·杜德纳和埃曼纽尔·夏彭蒂耶因开发CRISPR-Cas9——一种以前所未有的精度编辑DNA的革命性工具——而分享了2020年诺贝尔化学奖。她们的发现改变了生物学，使遗传疾病的潜在治愈、抗病作物的创造和治疗癌症的新方法成为可能。这是21世纪最具深远影响的科学发现之一。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (2020) for CRISPR-Cas9
Revolutionized genome editing
Founded the Innovative Genomics Institute
achievementsCn: 因CRISPR-Cas9获诺贝尔化学奖（2020年）
革新了基因组编辑
创立创新基因组学研究所
quote: The power to control our species' genetic future is awesome and terrifying.
quoteCn: 控制我们物种基因未来的力量既令人敬畏又令人恐惧。
references: Doudna, J. & Sternberg, S. A Crack in Creation. Houghton Mifflin, 2017.

name: Emmanuelle Charpentier
taglineCn: CRISPR基因编辑的共同发明者
bio: Emmanuelle Charpentier is a French microbiologist and biochemist who co-developed CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing technology with Jennifer Doudna. Her discovery of tracrRNA, a molecule essential for bacterial immune defense, was the key insight that led to the development of the CRISPR tool. She shared the 2020 Nobel Prize in Chemistry, making Doudna and Charpentier the first all-female team to share a science Nobel.
bioCn: 埃曼纽尔·夏彭蒂耶是一位法国微生物学家和生物化学家，与珍妮弗·杜德纳共同开发了CRISPR-Cas9基因编辑技术。她对tracrRNA——一种对细菌免疫防御至关重要的分子——的发现是开发CRISPR工具的关键洞见。她分享了2020年诺贝尔化学奖，使杜德纳和夏彭蒂耶成为第一个分享诺贝尔科学奖的全女性团队。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (2020) for CRISPR-Cas9
Discovered tracrRNA crucial for CRISPR mechanism
First all-female team to share a science Nobel Prize
achievementsCn: 因CRISPR-Cas9获诺贝尔化学奖（2020年）
发现对CRISPR机制至关重要的tracrRNA
第一个分享诺贝尔科学奖的全女性团队

name: Tu Youyou
bio: Tu Youyou discovered artemisinin, a compound derived from traditional Chinese medicine that has saved millions of lives from malaria. During the Cultural Revolution, she led a secret military project that screened thousands of herbal remedies. Inspired by a 1,600-year-old text by Ge Hong, she developed a low-temperature extraction method that preserved the compound's efficacy. She received the 2015 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine — the first Chinese Nobel laureate in the sciences and the first without a doctoral degree or overseas study experience.
bioCn: 屠呦呦发现了青蒿素——一种源自中国传统医学的化合物，已经拯救了数百万人免于疟疾。在文化大革命期间，她领导了一个秘密军事项目，筛选了数千种草药。受葛洪1600年前古籍的启发，她开发了保持化合物功效的低温提取方法。她获得了2015年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖——中国第一位科学领域的诺贝尔奖得主，也是第一位没有博士学位和海外留学经历的获奖者。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (2015)
Discovered artemisinin, saving millions from malaria
First Chinese scientist to win a Nobel Prize
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（2015年）
发现青蒿素，拯救数百万人免于疟疾
中国第一位获得诺贝尔奖的科学家
quote: Every scientist dreams of doing something that can help the world.
quoteCn: 每个科学家都梦想做一些能帮助世界的事情。

name: Frances Arnold
tagline: Nobel Laureate for Directed Evolution of Enzymes
taglineCn: 因酶的定向进化获诺贝尔奖
bio: Frances Arnold pioneered the use of directed evolution to engineer enzymes with new and useful properties, revolutionizing green chemistry and pharmaceutical manufacturing. She was awarded the 2018 Nobel Prize in Chemistry, becoming the fifth woman and the first American woman to win the prize. Her methods harness the power of natural selection to develop proteins that solve chemical problems, with applications ranging from biofuels to pharmaceuticals. A professor at Caltech, she has also co-founded several companies based on her technology.
bioCn: 弗朗西丝·阿诺德开创了利用定向进化来设计具有新的有用特性的酶的方法，彻底改变了绿色化学和制药行业。她因此获得2018年诺贝尔化学奖，成为第五位获此殊荣的女性，也是第一位获得诺贝尔化学奖的美国女性。她的方法利用自然选择的力量来开发能够解决化学问题的蛋白质，应用范围从生物燃料到药物制造。作为加州理工学院的教授，她还基于自己的技术共同创办了多家公司。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (2018) for directed evolution of enzymes
Pioneered methods to harness evolution for enzyme engineering
First American woman to win a Nobel Prize in Chemistry
achievementsCn: 因酶的定向进化获诺贝尔化学奖（2018年）
开创了利用进化原理进行酶工程改造的方法
第一位获得诺贝尔化学奖的美国女性

name: Ada Yonath
taglineCn: 因核糖体结构研究获诺贝尔奖
bio: Ada Yonath is an Israeli crystallographer who pioneered the study of ribosome structure using X-ray crystallography. She was the first scientist to successfully crystallize ribosomes, a feat once thought impossible, and spent over two decades mapping their atomic structure. Her work was foundational for understanding how antibiotics target bacterial ribosomes, opening new paths for drug development. She received the 2009 Nobel Prize in Chemistry, becoming the first Israeli woman to win a Nobel Prize and the first woman in 45 years to win the Chemistry Nobel.
bioCn: 阿达·约纳特是以色列晶体学家，她开创了利用X射线晶体学研究核糖体结构的先河。她是第一位成功使核糖体结晶的科学家——这一壮举曾被认为不可能——并花了二十多年绘制其原子结构图。她的工作对于理解抗生素如何靶向细菌核糖体至关重要，为药物开发开辟了新途径。她获得了2009年诺贝尔化学奖，成为第一位获得诺贝尔奖的以色列女性，也是45年来首位获得化学诺贝尔奖的女性。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (2009) for ribosome structure studies
First scientist to successfully crystallize ribosomes
First Israeli woman to win a Nobel Prize
achievementsCn: 因核糖体结构研究获诺贝尔化学奖（2009年）
第一位成功使核糖体结晶的科学家
第一位获得诺贝尔奖的以色列女性

name: Carolyn Bertozzi
taglineCn: 因生物正交化学获诺贝尔奖
bio: Carolyn Bertozzi developed bioorthogonal chemistry — chemical reactions that can occur inside living organisms without interfering with normal biological processes. Her work, alongside advances in click chemistry, earned her the 2022 Nobel Prize in Chemistry, shared with Morten Meldal and K. Barry Sharpless. Her innovations have enabled scientists to study cells and develop targeted pharmaceuticals in ways previously impossible. She is a professor at Stanford University and a Howard Hughes Medical Institute investigator.
bioCn: 卡罗琳·贝尔托齐开发了生物正交化学——一种可以在活体内进行而不干扰正常生物过程的化学反应。她的工作与点击化学的进展一起为她赢得了2022年诺贝尔化学奖，与莫滕·梅尔达尔和K·巴里·夏普利斯共享。她的创新使科学家能够以前所未有的方式研究细胞和开发靶向药物。她是斯坦福大学教授和霍华德·休斯医学研究所研究员。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (2022) for bioorthogonal and click chemistry
Developed chemical reactions that work inside living organisms
Stanford professor and Howard Hughes Medical Institute investigator
achievementsCn: 因生物正交化学和点击化学获诺贝尔化学奖（2022年）
开发了可在活体内进行的化学反应
斯坦福大学教授和霍华德·休斯医学研究所研究员

name: Marie Maynard Daly
tagline: First African American Woman to Earn a Chemistry PhD
taglineCn: 第一位获得化学博士学位的非裔美国女性
bio: Marie Maynard Daly became the first African American woman to earn a PhD in chemistry in the United States, graduating from Columbia University in 1947. Her groundbreaking research revealed the relationship between high cholesterol and clogged arteries, contributing significantly to the understanding of heart disease. She also studied the effects of aging, smoking, and hypertension on the lungs and circulatory system. Throughout her career, she worked tirelessly to increase enrollment of minority students in medical school and science graduate programs.
bioCn: 玛丽·梅纳德·戴利于1947年从哥伦比亚大学毕业，成为美国第一位获得化学博士学位的非裔美国女性。她的开创性研究揭示了高胆固醇与动脉堵塞之间的关系，对理解心脏病做出了重要贡献。她还研究了衰老、吸烟和高血压对肺部和循环系统的影响。在整个职业生涯中，她不懈努力，推动少数族裔学生进入医学院和科学研究生项目。
achievements: First African American woman to earn a PhD in chemistry in the U.S. (1947)
Revealed the link between high cholesterol and clogged arteries
Pioneered research on the effects of smoking and aging on the lungs
achievementsCn: 美国第一位获得化学博士学位的非裔美国女性（1947年）
揭示了高胆固醇与动脉堵塞之间的关系
开创了吸烟和衰老对肺部影响的研究
references: Brown, Jeannette E. African American Women Chemists. Oxford University Press, 2011.

name: Asima Chatterjee
tagline: Pioneer of Organic Chemistry in India
bio: Asima Chatterjee was a pioneering Indian organic chemist renowned for her extensive work on alkaloids and medicinal plants. She developed anti-epileptic and anti-malarial drugs derived from plant sources, making significant contributions to pharmaceutical chemistry. She was the first woman to receive a Doctor of Science degree from an Indian university and published extensively on the chemistry of plant-based compounds. A recipient of the Padma Bhushan, one of India's highest civilian honors, she mentored generations of chemists throughout her distinguished career.
bioCn: 阿西玛·查特吉是一位开创性的印度有机化学家，以对生物碱和药用植物的广泛研究而闻名。她从植物来源开发了抗癫痫和抗疟疾药物，对药物化学做出了重大贡献。她是第一位获得印度大学理学博士学位的女性，并在植物化合物化学方面发表了大量论著。她是印度最高平民荣誉之一莲花装勋章的获得者，在其杰出的职业生涯中培养了几代化学家。
achievements: Developed anti-epileptic and anti-malarial drugs from plant sources
First woman to receive a Doctor of Science from an Indian university
Padma Bhushan awardee for contributions to chemistry
achievementsCn: 从植物来源开发了抗癫痫和抗疟疾药物
第一位获得印度大学理学博士学位的女性
因对化学的贡献获莲花装勋章
references: Ray, P. History of Chemistry in Ancient and Medieval India. Indian Chemical Society, 1956.

name: Agnes Pockels
tagline: Pioneer of Surface Science from Her Kitchen Sink
taglineCn: 在厨房水槽旁开创表面科学的先驱
bio: Agnes Pockels conducted groundbreaking experiments on surface tension from her kitchen sink, having been denied university education as a woman in 19th-century Germany. She invented the slide trough, the precursor to the Langmuir-Blodgett trough that became essential to surface chemistry research. Unable to publish through traditional channels, she sent her findings to Lord Rayleigh, who was so impressed that he arranged for their publication in Nature in 1891. Her self-taught discoveries laid the foundation for modern surface science.
bioCn: 阿格内斯·波克尔斯在她的厨房水槽旁进行了关于表面张力的开创性实验——在19世纪的德国，她因为是女性而被拒绝接受大学教育。她发明了滑动槽，即后来成为表面化学研究必备工具的朗缪尔-布洛杰特槽的前身。由于无法通过传统渠道发表成果，她将研究寄给了瑞利勋爵，后者非常赞赏，安排于1891年在《自然》杂志上发表。她自学成才的发现奠定了现代表面科学的基础。
achievements: Pioneered surface tension measurements at her kitchen sink
Invented the slide trough, precursor to the Langmuir-Blodgett trough
Published in Nature through Lord Rayleigh's support (1891)
achievementsCn: 在厨房水槽旁开创了表面张力测量
发明了滑动槽，朗缪尔-布洛杰特槽的前身
通过瑞利勋爵的支持在《自然》杂志上发表（1891年）
references: Derrick, M.E. 'Agnes Pockels, 1862–1935.' Journal of Chemical Education, 1982.

name: Clara Immerwahr
tagline: First Woman PhD in Chemistry from Breslau
taglineCn: 布雷斯劳大学第一位化学女博士
bio: Clara Immerwahr became the first woman to earn a PhD in chemistry from the University of Breslau in 1900, a remarkable achievement for that era. She married Fritz Haber, who would go on to develop chemical weapons for the German military. Immerwahr was a vocal opponent of chemical weapons research, viewing it as a perversion of science. After the first deployment of chlorine gas at the Second Battle of Ypres in April 1915, she took her own life in protest — a tragic act that has made her a symbol of the ethical responsibilities of scientists.
bioCn: 克拉拉·伊默瓦尔于1900年成为布雷斯劳大学第一位获得化学博士学位的女性，这在那个时代是非凡的成就。她嫁给了后来为德国军方开发化学武器的弗里茨·哈伯。伊默瓦尔强烈反对化学武器研究，认为这是对科学的亵渎。1915年4月，在第二次伊普尔战役中首次使用氯气后，她以自杀的方式表达抗议——这一悲剧性行为使她成为科学家伦理责任的象征。
achievements: First woman to earn a PhD in chemistry from the University of Breslau (1900)
Vocal opponent of chemical weapons development
Symbol of ethical responsibility in science
achievementsCn: 布雷斯劳大学第一位化学女博士（1900年）
化学武器开发的坚定反对者
科学伦理责任的象征
references: Friedrich, Bretislav. 'Clara Haber, nee Immerwahr: Life, Work, and Legacy.' Zeitschrift für anorganische und allgemeine Chemie, 2015.

name: Mildred Cohn
tagline: Pioneer of NMR Spectroscopy in Biochemistry
taglineCn: 生物化学核磁共振波谱学的先驱
bio: Mildred Cohn was an American biochemist who pioneered the use of nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) spectroscopy and isotope tracers to study enzyme mechanisms, transforming our understanding of how enzymes catalyze biological reactions. She received the National Medal of Science in 1982 and was the first woman elected to the board of directors of the American Chemical Society. As a professor at the University of Pennsylvania, she overcame significant gender discrimination throughout her career, having been denied teaching positions and promotions simply because she was a woman.
bioCn: 米尔德里德·科恩是一位美国生物化学家，她开创了利用核磁共振（NMR）波谱学和同位素示踪剂研究酶机制的方法，深刻改变了我们对酶如何催化生物反应的理解。她于1982年获得国家科学奖章，并且是第一位当选美国化学学会董事会成员的女性。作为宾夕法尼亚大学教授，她在整个职业生涯中克服了严重的性别歧视——仅仅因为是女性就被拒绝教职和晋升机会。
achievements: Pioneered NMR spectroscopy for studying enzyme mechanisms
National Medal of Science (1982)
First woman on the board of directors of the American Chemical Society
achievementsCn: 开创了利用NMR波谱学研究酶机制的方法
国家科学奖章（1982年）
第一位当选美国化学学会董事会成员的女性
references: University of Pennsylvania: Mildred Cohn memorial — https://www.med.upenn.edu/

name: Isabella Karle
tagline: X-ray Crystallographer Who Decoded Molecular Structures
taglineCn: 解码分子结构的X射线晶体学家
bio: Isabella Karle was an American X-ray crystallographer at the Naval Research Laboratory who developed practical methods for determining molecular structures from X-ray diffraction data. Her techniques are now used worldwide in drug design, materials science, and biological research. She received the National Medal of Science and numerous other honors. Working alongside her husband Jerome Karle, she developed the experimental methods that complemented his theoretical framework — work for which he shared the 1985 Nobel Prize in Chemistry, though Isabella's essential contributions were not included in the award.
bioCn: 伊莎贝拉·卡尔勒是美国海军研究实验室的X射线晶体学家，她开发了从X射线衍射数据确定分子结构的实用方法。她的技术如今在全球范围内被广泛用于药物设计、材料科学和生物学研究。她获得了国家科学奖章和众多其他荣誉。她与丈夫杰罗姆·卡尔勒并肩工作，开发了补充其理论框架的实验方法——杰罗姆因此分享了1985年诺贝尔化学奖，但伊莎贝拉的关键贡献却未被纳入奖项。
achievements: Developed practical methods for determining molecular structures
National Medal of Science recipient
Techniques used worldwide in drug design and materials science
achievementsCn: 开发了确定分子结构的实用方法
国家科学奖章获得者
技术在全球药物设计和材料科学中广泛使用
imageCredit: US Naval Research Laboratory, Public Domain
references: Naval Research Laboratory: Isabella Karle memorial

name: Jacqueline Barton
tagline: Discovered That DNA Conducts Electrical Charge
bio: Jacqueline Barton is an American chemist at the California Institute of Technology who made the remarkable discovery that DNA can conduct electrical charge over long distances. Her groundbreaking work on DNA-mediated charge transport has profound implications for understanding how cells detect and repair genetic damage. She received the National Medal of Science in 2011 and the Priestley Medal in 2015 — the highest honor of the American Chemical Society. Her research bridges inorganic chemistry, biochemistry, and molecular biology in pioneering ways.
bioCn: 杰奎琳·巴顿是加州理工学院的美国化学家，她做出了DNA能够远距离传导电荷的重大发现。她在DNA介导的电荷传输方面的开创性工作对理解细胞如何检测和修复基因损伤具有深远意义。她于2011年获得国家科学奖章，2015年获得普里斯特利奖章——美国化学学会的最高荣誉。她的研究以开创性的方式将无机化学、生物化学和分子生物学联系起来。
achievements: Discovered that DNA conducts electrical charge
National Medal of Science (2011)
Priestley Medal, highest honor of the American Chemical Society (2015)
achievementsCn: 发现DNA能传导电荷
国家科学奖章（2011年）
普里斯特利奖章，美国化学学会最高荣誉（2015年）
